stories.headline: ‘Humiliation’: Donald Trump battles claims his Iran deal is worse than Obama’s
stories.summary: US president’s critics ask if concessions to Tehran in purported agreement were worth four months of war

stories.headline: Iran war live: Israel kills 4 in Lebanon as Trump criticises Netanyahu
stories.summary: Warning comes as Trump criticises Netanyahu over Israel's attacks on Lebanon.

stories.headline: US Set to Offer Iran Broad Financial Gains in Peace Deal
stories.summary: Iran is set to receive broad financial incentives as part of its agreement with the US, including the right to sell oil immediately, according to a final draft of the deal. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports from Dubai. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: 'It was surreal': British couple describe having warning shots fired near them by Russian warship
stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: AI Could Create a New Inequality Crisis, Says IMF Chief
stories.summary: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva warns that artificial intelligence could deepen inequality if leaders fail to ensure its benefits are widely shared. She discusses with Francine Lacqua the impact of AI on jobs, productivity and financial stability, and explains why governments still have the tools to shape how technology transforms society. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil Tankers U-Turn, Rush to Middle East Before Hormuz Reopening
stories.summary: Two oil tankers heading toward Africa have u-turned in the Indian Ocean this week, switching their destinations to the Middle East as shipowners race to re-position vessels ahead of the possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Oil sinks to three-month low on hopes of Hormuz reopening
stories.summary: Oil prices fell below $80 per barrel as markets grew increasingly confident that the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is coming to an end, paving the way for a recovery in supplies from one of the world's most important oil-exporting regions.

stories.headline: Ambani set to fire starting gun on India’s largest IPO
stories.summary: Iran war has hit country’s equity market and disrupted what was positioned to be another record year for flotations

stories.headline: China’s Zhipu Gets Lone ‘Short’ Call After 1,100% Rally
stories.summary: Despite Zhipu being the hottest stock on the HSTECH Index this year, Felix Wang of Hedgeye Risk Management says the fair value for the stock on the short side is HK$407. He adds that Zhipu lacks pricing power, particularly after the release of DeepSeek’s V4 model, which has triggered a price war among Chinese AI firms. He spoke on “Bloomberg: The China Show.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Tim Fleming Wins GOP Runoff for Georgia Secretary of State
stories.summary: State Representative Tim Fleming defeated an ardent denier of the 2020 election results, Vernon Jones, in the Republican runoff to be Georgia’s top election official.

stories.headline: Lib Dems to urge Labour to drop ‘torpor and timidity’ on EU and rejoin single market
stories.summary: Exclusive: Ed Davey to make call ahead of 10th anniversary of Brexit vote, in strengthening of party’s position on EUThe Lib Dems will urge Andy Burnham to end Labour’s “torpor and timidity” towards the EU as they call for the UK to rejoin the single market, in a notable strengthening of their own position.Ahead of the 10th anniversary of the Brexit vote next week, Ed Davey will challenge Burnham to scrap Labour’s red lines on the customs union a

stories.headline: Japan ride-hailing app Go races toward robotaxis after successful IPO
G7 leaders press Trump on Ukraine as summit wraps up
stories.summary: The eyes of the world are on Evian-les-Bains, where G7 leaders are gathering for their third day and final day of talks. Beyond the photo ops, European leaders used this precious face time with the US President to urge him to break the deadlock on Ukraine.

stories.headline: G7 summit: Leaders commit to 'unwavering support' for Ukraine
stories.summary: The third and final day of the G7 summit in Évian, France, is underway. Last night, the leaders issued a joint declaration reiterating their "unwavering support for Ukraine in defending its freedom, sovereignty, and territorial integrity."

stories.headline: AI takes centre stage at G7 as Western fears over US ‘kill switch’ get real
stories.summary: Western allies arrived at the G7 AI summit unsettled after the Trump administration cut off foreign access to Anthropic's most powerful models, confirming Europe's kill-switch fears and providing yet another boost for its tech sovereignty agenda.

stories.headline: Extraordinary Messi makes more history in masterclass for the ages
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland both enjoyed a fine start to the World Cup - but on a day packed with superstars, Lionel Messi still took top billing.

stories.headline: Investors pile into bullish dollar bets as ‘US exceptionalism’ trade returns
stories.summary: Traders expect buoyant American economy to keep Fed from cutting rates despite oil price fall

stories.headline: Iron Ore Sinks Back Below $100 as Abundant Supplies Hurt Outlook
stories.summary: Iron ore sank below $100 a ton for the first time since March on signs of ample supplies just as demand in China faces headwinds.

stories.headline: BRICS Bank Lends South Africa $1 Billion for Cities, Report Says
stories.summary: New Development Bank is lending South Africa $1 billion for the state to upgrade infrastructure in its eight biggest cities, Business Day reported.

stories.headline: GLP-1 Craze Fuels Protein-Powder Maker Glanbia’s Share Rally
stories.summary: The boom in GLP-1 weight-loss drugs is boosting demand for protein supplements and driving a rally in the stock of Glanbia Plc, a maker of nutrition products popular with gym enthusiasts and athletes.

stories.headline: Australian LNG Union Reaches Deal to End Strikes at Ichthys
stories.summary: Unions in Australia and Japan’s Inpex Corp. agreed to end strikes at the Ichthys liquefied natural gas export facility after weeks of industrial action.

stories.headline: PBOC Launches Tool to Boost Yuan Use by Other Central Banks
stories.summary: China announced new money-market measures to broaden the use of its currency among entities like foreign central banks and sovereign wealth funds.

stories.headline: SNB Reviews Franc Alert Level Before Middle East Peace Deal
stories.summary: The Swiss National Bank will this week reveal if it’s too soon to dial down its franc rhetoric before a prospective Middle East peace deal becomes reality.

stories.headline: China’s Regulator to Support Launch of Active ETF Products
stories.summary: China’s regulator gave the greenlight for the rollout of actively managed exchange-traded funds, expanding the range of investment products available in the world’s second‑largest stock market and bringing it closer in line with more developed peers.

stories.headline: China Watchdog Urges More IPOs From AI, Hong Kong Listed Firms
stories.summary: China’s top securities regulator pledged to welcome more domestic listings from artificial intelligence developers as well as Hong Kong traded firms to bolster onshore capital markets.

stories.headline: Qatar Moves LNG Ships Back to Mideast Ahead of Hormuz Reopening
stories.summary: Qatar is beginning to bring some of its liquefied natural gas tankers back to the Middle East, as the major supplier prepares to ramp-up exports once the Strait of Hormuz reopens following a US-Iran deal.

stories.headline: Still See More Upside to Tech Trade: JPMorgan Asset Management
stories.summary: Aisa Ogoshi of JPMorgan Asset Management says "risk obviously is in the tech trade" due to the market concentration. But she adds that "there's more upside to the tech trade" in terms of the AI data center supply chain. Ogoshi also discusses the outlook for the Bank of Japan. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Georgia Vexes Trump Yet Again: 6 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primaries
stories.summary: Lt. Gov. Burt Jones lost the Republican runoff for governor to the health care executive Rick Jackson despite the president’s endorsement. Mr. Trump’s picks won in other races.

stories.headline: Rep. Barry Moore Wins Senate Primary in Alabama, Holding Off Challenger
stories.summary: The contest to fill Alabama’s open Senate seat had become closer than expected between the House lawmaker and Jared Hudson, a political newcomer.

stories.headline: Attorney General and Businessman Make Oklahoma Governor’s Runoff
stories.summary: Gentner Drummond, the attorney general, and Mike Mazzei, a newcomer who secured President Trump’s endorsement, emerged from a crowded field in the Republican primary.

stories.headline: Trump Officials Challenge Reparations Program in Chicago Suburb
stories.summary: The Justice Department argued that the program, which seeks to compensate Black residents for housing discrimination, was racist and unconstitutional.

stories.headline: Analysts predict no US petrol price drop until 2027
stories.summary: US drivers may have to wait until 2027 for gasoline prices to fall

stories.headline: How past ‘terrorist’ movements are often judged right by history
stories.summary: Who decides which movements are remembered as terrorists and which are celebrated as agents of change?

stories.headline: Vozinha’s mum celebrates hero keeper son from afar
stories.summary: The mother of Cape Verde’s celebrated goalkeeper Vozinha says she watched his heroics on the TV.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

stories.headline: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.headline: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’

stories.headline: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
stories.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

stories.headline: Iran deal presents political nightmare for Netanyahu
stories.summary: Donald Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran leaves the Israeli PM trapped in a new political and security dilemma.

stories.headline: Oil Tumbles to Fresh Three-Month Low on Hormuz Reopening Trade
stories.summary: Oil tumbled to a fresh three-month low on gathering expectations that a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz waterway will unleash a wave of supply, loosening the global crude market.

stories.headline: Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild power grid
stories.summary: The deal with General Electric is the latest sign of co-operation between US firms and Venezuela's interim government.

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
stories.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

stories.headline: India temporarily bans Telegram over exam paper leak concerns
stories.summary: The NEET exam had earlier been scrapped over allegations of a paper leak, sparking massive protests.

stories.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

stories.headline: Ex-Citi executive says bank sacked her after raising Trump concerns
stories.summary: Former employee claims in lawsuit that she identified issues with risk-management practices

stories.headline: Philippines Raises $2.5 Billion in Its Second Bond Deal of 2026
stories.summary: The Philippines raised $2.5 billion from its second international bond sale of the year, seizing the opportunity to finance government spending as borrowing costs ease amid optimism on a potential US-Iran peace agreement.

stories.headline: China Stock Gauge Sinks as Traders Favor AI Winners Elsewhere
stories.summary: Chinese stocks listed in Hong Kong are facing bleak milestones as a global rush into artificial intelligence supply chain players sidelines the Internet and consumer companies that dominate the offshore benchmark.

stories.headline: Bonds Rally Before Fed as Oil Extends Losing Run: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Bonds climbed as oil headed for its longest losing streak in 10 months, with investors awaiting the Federal Reserve’s policy decision for fresh clues on the outlook for global interest rates.

stories.headline: America’s bull market has entered its manic phase
stories.summary: Options markets show optimism is giving way to euphoria

stories.headline: US and Iran agree deal to end war as Trump says Strait of Hormuz to reopen
stories.summary: Both sides declare "the immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon", Pakistan says.

stories.headline: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
stories.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

stories.headline: Trump aims to end Iran war but nuclear issue remains unresolved
stories.summary: President Trump says the US and Iran have agreed a deal to end the war but it is unclear what has been agreed on Iran's nuclear programme

stories.headline: Political tension has undermined World Cup joy, says Iran striker
stories.summary: Iran's striker Mehdi Taremi said the country's political predicament "undermines the joy of the World Cup".

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.

stories.headline: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies falling into volcanic crater
stories.summary: Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar had been attempting to climb its walls without safety equipment when he fell in, local authorities say.

stories.headline: South Korea's Starbucks to shut for staff history lesson after backlash
stories.summary: The chain was criticised for a "Tank Day" marketing ploy on the anniversary of a military massacre.

stories.headline: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies

stories.headline: Israeli military says Hezbollah targets struck in Beirut
stories.summary: Israel says it targeted the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Tehran warns this could derail a US-Iran deal to end the fighting.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
stories.summary: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

stories.headline: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
stories.summary: Japanese firms will spend billions on UK infrastructure and offshore wind, Downing Street says.

stories.headline: Israel carries out air strikes on Lebanon, state media says, as Iran claims deal with US near
stories.summary: The strikes come as Iran's foreign minister says a deal to end fighting with the US is close.

stories.headline: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
stories.summary: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

stories.headline: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online
stories.summary: A niche group of content creators called "virtual parents" have become hugely popular among young people.

stories.headline: The college scam that promised students fleeing war a new life in Finland
stories.summary: BBC speaks to students from war-torn Myanmar who say they were cheated after paying to study abroad.

stories.headline: Watch: Fans celebrate Mexico's win in World Cup opening match
stories.summary: There were wild celebrations in host nation Mexico, as their team kicked off their World Cup campaign with a 2-0 win over South Africa.

stories.headline: Israeli tech firm accused of targeting First Minister in election
stories.summary: Accounts linked to tech firm BlackCore are alleged to have carried out 'digital interference operations' against John Swinney.

stories.headline: London council seizes social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone first lady
